content.text: The Role of Facilitation Artefacts in Claude Discovery

A discovery workshop without durable artefacts is a conversation. Artefacts turn a conversation into a decision record: who was in the room, what was agreed, what was deferred, and what the output became. For Claude feature development this matters because the tacit knowledge generated in a 90-minute session is what bridges the gap between a product idea and a correctly-specified engineering task.
templates, facilitation guides, and hand-off documents

that make a discovery workshop reproducible. They are designed to be stored in the repository alongside the code they produced — so future engineers can trace every design decision back to the session that produced it.
A template is a promise to your future self: 'The next person who runs this workshop will not have to reinvent it from scratch.'

attrs.alt: Discovery workshop lifecycle from pre-workshop preparation through the live session, synthesis, and artefact hand-off to engineering and legal stakeholders

content.text: The four-phase lifecycle above shows how preparation, facilitation, synthesis, and hand-off connect. Each phase has concrete deliverables; the next phase cannot begin without them. This constraint prevents the most common failure mode: a workshop with great energy but no durable output.
The scope document is sent to all participants five days before the session. It frames the feature, defines what is in scope, and sets expectations. It must be short enough to read in five minutes.

# Discovery Workshop: [Feature Name]
**Date/Time:** [datetime]  **Location / Video link:** [link]  **Facilitator:** [name]

## Feature Brief (2–3 sentences)
Describe what the Claude feature does, who uses it, and why it matters now.

## Participants and Their Roles
| Name | Role | What they bring |
|------|------|-----------------|
| | Product Manager | Owns scope decisions; breaks ties |
| | Senior Engineer | Surfaces technical constraints |
| | ML Engineer | Flags model-specific limitations |
| | Domain Expert | Validates correctness of domain events |
| | Legal / Compliance | Flags policy and data-handling constraints |
| | Facilitator | Runs the session; does not add stickies |

## In Scope
- [Specific user journey or feature boundary to explore]

## Out of Scope
- [Explicit exclusions to prevent session sprawl]

## Pre-Session Homework (required, 20 min)
Open [digital board link] and add 10 orange sticky notes — domain events you
expect to find in this feature. Past tense, e.g. "QueryReceived".

## Session Output
By T+48 hours you will receive:
1. Domain event glossary
2. Bounded context map
3. Draft backlog epics with acceptance criteria
4. Risk log (hot spots)
Sticky-Note Primer (1 Page — Shared Before the Session)

# Event Storming Quick Reference

## The 7 Note Types
| Colour | Type | Rule |
|--------|------|------|
| 🟠 Orange | Domain Event | Past tense. Something that happened. "ResponseStreamed" |
| 🔵 Blue | Command | Present tense imperative. "GenerateResponse" |
| 🟣 Purple | Policy / Rule | "Whenever [event], then [command]" |
| 🟡 Yellow | Query / Read Model | Data the actor reads before issuing the command |
| 🔴 Red | Hot Spot | Open question or disagreement. Ask, don't resolve now |
| 🟢 Green | External System | Third-party or separate service |
| 🟤 Brown | Aggregate | Logical boundary around related events |

## The 3 Rules
1. **No solution stickies on orange notes.** Events describe what happened, not how.
2. **One idea per note.** If you need two lines, it is two notes.
3. **Red notes are productive.** Uncertainty surfaced is a risk mitigated.
The facilitation guide is the script the facilitator follows during the session. It should be visible only to the facilitator — not projected on screen.

=== CLAUDE FEATURE DISCOVERY — FACILITATION GUIDE ===

PRE-SESSION CHECKLIST (15 min before start)
□ Digital board open with template loaded
□ Async pre-work stickies visible but un-grouped
□ Timer app ready (recommend: timer.guru)
□ Recording consent confirmed with attendees
□ Green (external system) stickies pre-populated: Anthropic API, Auth Provider,
  any known MCP tool servers, analytics pipeline

--- 0:00 FRAMING (5 min) ---
"Welcome. Today we have 90 minutes to map every domain event in [Feature Name].
By the end we will know:
  1. What this feature does (event timeline)
  2. Where it fails (hot spots)
  3. Where the service boundaries are (bounded contexts)

I will not be adding sticky notes. My job is to keep us moving.
If we can't agree in 90 seconds, it becomes a red hot-spot note and we move on."

--- 0:05 CHAOTIC STORM (20 min) ---
"Write every domain event you can think of for this feature. Orange notes.
Past tense. One per note. Post them on the timeline without discussing.
Go."
[Timer: 20 min. Walk the room. Prompt stragglers: 'What happens after that?']

--- 0:25 NARRATIVE WALK (20 min) ---
"Now we walk the timeline together. I'll point to events, we'll add commands,
policies, queries, and external systems."
[Point left to right. For each cluster:]
  → "What triggered this?" [add blue]
  → "What rule or policy applies?" [add purple]
  → "What does the actor need to read first?" [add yellow]
  → "Any external system involved?" [add green]
  → "Disagree? 90 seconds, then red."

--- 0:45 AGGREGATE IDENTIFICATION (20 min) ---
"Draw lines around events that share a consistency boundary. If they always
fail together, they're in the same aggregate. Brown note = aggregate name."

--- 1:05 BOUNDED CONTEXT MAPPING (15 min) ---
"Group aggregates into service boundaries. Each context will likely become
a separate deployment unit. Name them and draw lines."

--- 1:20 HOT-SPOT VOTE (10 min) ---
"Give everyone 3 dot stickers. Vote for the hot spots most critical to resolve
before sprint planning. Top 3 become spike stories."

--- 1:30 CLOSE ---
"Photo or export the board. I'll send the synthesised artefacts in 24 hours.
Thank you."

# Domain Event Glossary — [Feature Name]
_Generated from Event Storming session on [date]. Do not edit manually —
update by running a new session and replacing this file._

## Events by Bounded Context

### Conversation Context
| Event | Trigger | Payload (key fields) | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------------------|-------|
| QueryReceived | User submits message | user_id, session_id, text, timestamp | Entry point to the feature |
| IntentClassified | Pre-classification LLM call | intent_label, confidence_score | Routes to model or clarification |
| ResponseStreamed | Generation complete | token_count, latency_ms, model_id | Stream token-by-token to client |
| ToolCallExecuted | Agent invokes MCP tool | tool_name, params, result_summary | Async; may fire multiple times |

### Safety & Policy Context
| Event | Trigger | Payload (key fields) | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------------------|-------|
| GuardrailTripped | Content policy check fails | rule_id, severity, action_taken | Blocks or modifies response |
| AuditLogWritten | Any policy event | event_type, actor_id, timestamp | Immutable; append-only |

### Evaluation & Feedback Context
| Event | Trigger | Payload (key fields) | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------------------|-------|
| FeedbackCaptured | User rates response | rating, session_id, response_id | Drives eval dataset |
| EvalScoreRecorded | Automated eval run | metric_name, score, model_version | Persisted for trend analysis |

## Naming Conventions
- Events: PascalCase past tense noun phrase.
- Commands: PascalCase imperative verb phrase.
- Aggregates: PascalCase singular noun.

# DR-[NNN]: [Decision Title]
**Date:** [date]  **Status:** Accepted  **Deciders:** [names and roles]

## Context
What situation or hot spot prompted this decision?

## Decision
State the decision in one sentence.

## Rationale
Why this option over alternatives considered?

## Alternatives Considered
| Option | Why rejected |
|--------|-------------|
| | |

## Consequences
- Positive: 
- Negative / trade-offs:
- Requires: (follow-on decisions or spike stories)

## Review Trigger
When should this decision be revisited? (e.g., "if p99 latency exceeds 4 s")

The synthesis phase — converting a raw event board into a glossary, risk log, backlog, and decision records — is time-consuming when done manually. Claude can accelerate this if the board is exported as structured JSON (Miro and FigJam both support this).

import anthropic, json
from pathlib import Path

def synthesise_workshop_artefacts(
    board_export: dict,  # JSON export from Miro or FigJam
    feature_name: str,
    output_dir: str = "docs/discovery"
) -> dict[str, str]:
    """
    Convert a raw Event Storming board export into structured artefacts.
    Returns a dict of {filename: content} for writing to disk.
    """
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    # Extract sticky notes by colour/type from the board export
    stickies_by_type = _parse_board_export(board_export)

    artefacts = {}

    # 1. Domain Event Glossary
    glossary_response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=2000,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"""
Feature: {feature_name}

Domain events from Event Storming board:
{json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("events", []), indent=2)}

External systems identified:
{json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("systems", []), indent=2)}

Aggregates identified:
{json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("aggregates", []), indent=2)}

Generate a domain event glossary as a markdown table grouped by bounded context.
Include columns: Event, Trigger, Key Payload Fields, Notes."""}]
    )
    artefacts["domain-event-glossary.md"] = glossary_response.content[0].text

    # 2. Risk Log from Hot Spots
    if stickies_by_type.get("hotspots"):
        risk_response = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=1500,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"""
Hot spots (unresolved questions) from Event Storming:
{json.dumps(stickies_by_type["hotspots"], indent=2)}

For each hot spot, generate a risk log entry with:
- Risk description
- Probability (Low/Medium/High)
- Impact (Low/Medium/High)
- Proposed mitigation
- Owner role

Format as a markdown table."""}]
        )
        artefacts["risk-log.md"] = risk_response.content[0].text

    # 3. Draft Backlog Epics
    backlog_response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=2000,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"""
Feature: {feature_name}

Bounded contexts: {json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("contexts", []), indent=2)}
Commands: {json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("commands", []), indent=2)}
Key domain events: {json.dumps(stickies_by_type.get("events", [])[:20], indent=2)}

Generate 3-5 draft backlog epics. For each epic:
- Title (one line)
- User story ("As a ... I want ... so that ...")
- 3 acceptance criteria (Given/When/Then)
- Story point estimate (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13)

Format as markdown."""}]
    )
    artefacts["draft-backlog.md"] = backlog_response.content[0].text

    # Write all artefacts to disk
    Path(output_dir).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    for filename, content in artefacts.items():
        Path(f"{output_dir}/{filename}").write_text(content)

    return artefacts


def _parse_board_export(board_export: dict) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
    """Extract sticky notes by type from a Miro/FigJam JSON export."""
    # Miro uses 'style.fillColor' to identify sticky type
    # This mapping matches the standard Event Storming colour palette
    colour_to_type = {
        "#ff9800": "events",    # orange → domain events
        "#2196f3": "commands",  # blue → commands
        "#9c27b0": "policies",  # purple → policies
        "#ffeb3b": "queries",   # yellow → queries/read models
        "#f44336": "hotspots",  # red → hot spots
        "#4caf50": "systems",   # green → external systems
        "#795548": "aggregates", # brown → aggregates
    }
    result = {v: [] for v in colour_to_type.values()}
    for widget in board_export.get("widgets", []):
        if widget.get("type") == "STICKY_NOTE":
            colour = widget.get("style", {}).get("fillColor", "").lower()
            note_type = colour_to_type.get(colour)
            if note_type:
                result[note_type].append(widget.get("text", "").strip())
    return result
The hand-off package is due 48 hours after the session. The facilitator owns delivery; the product manager owns completeness review.
Each hot spot has a named owner and next action
Committed to repo, tagged with session date
Uploaded within 2 hours of session close

Templates should be treated as living documents. After each discovery workshop, the facilitator adds a retrospective note to the scope document covering three questions: what worked, what was confusing, and what would be changed for the next session. These notes accumulate into an organisational playbook over time.
: each update to a template file is a commit with a message explaining why the change was made.
: gather all facilitators who have run sessions in the past 90 days and compare retrospective notes. Update templates based on shared patterns.

: after each sprint planning meeting that follows a discovery workshop, ask the engineering lead: 'Did the artefacts give you enough to plan without follow-up questions?' A 'yes' rate below 80% indicates a template gap.

Workshop facilitation artefacts transform a discovery session into a traceable, reproducible process. The pre-workshop scope document and sticky-note primer set expectations. The facilitation guide gives the facilitator a reliable 90-minute script. The synthesis templates — domain event glossary, decision records, risk log, and draft backlog — ensure that the session output reaches engineers, legal, and product stakeholders in a form they can act on immediately. Automating synthesis with Claude accelerates the T+24-hour artefact delivery deadline and ensures consistent formatting. Versioning templates in git and iterating based on retrospective feedback builds an organisational capability that improves with every workshop run.
